content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMIVIISSION
P.O. Box 807
. 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
. Fax (916) 322-0886
September29, 1998
Sue Lasher
Santa Clarans for Political Integrity
and Ethics
P.O. Box 483
Santa Clara, CA 95052
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-98-233
Dear Ms. Lasher:
This is in response to your request for advice under the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the “Act).’ Since you are merely aslcing a general reporting question,
your letter is considered a request for informal assistance.
2
QUESTION
Prior to 1995, your organization was a local government “watchdog” group. In 1995, the
organization qualified as a campaign committee when it attempted the recall of a local city
council member. The recall failed. May you now terminate the committee’s filing obligations
and return it to its original status?
CONCLUSION
Yes. If your organization is no longer engaged in the type of activity which would
qualifi it as a campaign committee under the provisions of the Act, it may terminate its filing
obligations and resume its role as a local government “watchdog” group.
FACTS AND ANALYSIS
Santa Clarans for Political Integrity and Ethics was originally formed as a local
government “watchdog” group in the City of Santa Clara. In 1995, the group attempted a recall
‘Government Code Section 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18100-18995,
of the California Code ofRegulations.
2
Jorm1 assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)


File No. 1-98-233
Page 2
of a city council member. Your group raised money for the recall effort and qualified as a
campaign committee with reporting duties under the provisions ofthe Act. The recall attempt
failed. Santa Clarans for Political Integrity and Ethics has not engaged in making contributions
or expenditures on behalf of candidates or measures since the 1995 recall attempt. The
organization would like to resume its role as a local government “watchdog” group and terminate
its campaign filing obligations.
Your status as a committee may be terminated once you have ceased to engage in the type
of activity which required the filing of campaign statements under the provisions ofthe Act. If
that is true for your committee, the committee treasurer may terminate the committee by filing an
original Form 415, Recipient Committee Statement of Termination with the Secretary of State.
A copy ofthe Form 415, along with an original Form 450, Recipient Committee Short Form
3
must be filed with the city clerk for the city of Santa Clara. On the Form 415, termination
statement, the treasurer is verifying that the committee has ceased to receive contributions and
make expenditures; does not anticipate receiving contributions or making expenditures in the
future; has eliminated or declares that it has no intention or ability to discharge all debts, loans,
and other obligations (if any); has no surplus funds; and has filed all campaign statements
required by the Political Reform Act disclosing all reportable transactions.
On the Form 450, the Recipient Committee Short Form, the committee needs to report on
Part IV where its remaining funds will be disbursed. Any expenditure by the committee must be
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. If it is the intent ofthe
organization to resume its role as a local government “watchdog” group, the expenditure ofthe
committee funds to support this role, is reasonably related to a governmental purpose. After
completing Part IV indicating the disbursement of funds to the original local government
watchdog group, be sure to complete the summary page portion of the Form 450, showing your
beginning balance, contributions received, and expenditures made. Your ending cash balance
should be a zero.
I trust this answers your question sufficiently. If you need any assistance in completing
the termination forms, please be sure to call the Commission’s Technical Assistance Division at
(916) 322-5660 for further assistance.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
3
1n your letter you indicated that the only money which you have raised over the past couple of years is
$10 per member annually and that the group has not supported any candidates or ballot measures during this time.
Since you have not received any coniributions which must be itemized, that is, $100 or more, the Form 450 may be
used in place of the Form 419, Ballot Measure Statement, long form, which you may have used in the past.
